segments.text: After years of rivalry, Apple just picked Google to power series AI. For the past 2 years, Apple has been quietly testing AI models from OpenAI,
Anthropic, and Google. They needed a foundational model powerful enough to overhaul Siri, something they've delayed multiple times. After testing all three,
Apple chose Gemini. Now, Apple already works with OpenAI. Chart GPT is integrated into Siri for complex queries, but that partnership is for
specific tasks. This Google deal is foundational, meaning Gemini powers the entire next version of Siri launching
later this year. Google already pays Apple around $20 billion per year to be the default search engine on iPhones.
Now, as per reports, Apple will pay Google $1 billion per year to power Siri. Just days ago, Google briefly
touched a $4 trillion market cap driven largely by its AI momentum, cloud growth, and enterprise adoption. Now,
here's where it gets uncomfortable. In August 2024, a federal judge ruled it illegally maintained a search monopoly,
partly through deals like the one with Apple. And this deal might dent Apple's reputation. What do you think? Let me know in the comments.
